metadata.title: Three Files Before Claude Touches Anything
metadata.description_blurb: When Claude picks the aesthetic defaults, the work is polished but belongs to nobody — three files written before Claude touches anything keep every creative decision traceable to a human choice.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B06: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: He asked Claude to write a system prompt for a senior game designer agent. Claude returned a warm persona with encouraging language, three example commands, and a friendly escalation path. It was well-crafted. He showed it to his professor. She asked: which of these decisions did you make? He could not answer. The tone, the command structure, the escalation path — Claude had picked all of it. He had received a product. He had not built one. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, system prompt for game designer agent

props.command: write me a system prompt for a senior game designer agent

beats.narration_text: When Claude picks aesthetic defaults, the output is polished and generic. Polished because Claude has read thousands of examples. Generic because the choices were not yours. The U.S. Copyright Office's 2023 guidance on AI-generated work identifies expressive choices made by the human author as the test for authorship. If Claude made the choices, the work passes the fluency test and fails the authorship test.
beats.new_visual_element: Received not made — Claude defaults are generic / Copyright Office 2023 / expressive choices = authorship

beats.narration_text: The Brutalist framework runs on three files written before Claude touches anything. CLAUDE.md: behavioral constraints — what Claude may and may not do, escalation rules. DESIGN.md: every aesthetic decision named — tone, register, what silence means. PROJECT.md intent layer: five questions answered in the human's voice — who is this for, what should they feel, what does it refuse, what is done, what is out of scope.
beats.new_visual_element: Three files — CLAUDE.md behavior / DESIGN.md aesthetics / PROJECT.md intent / all written before generation

beats.narration_text: The intent layer of PROJECT.md cannot be delegated. Five questions answered in your voice before Claude sees the project: Who is this for? What should they feel? What does this refuse? What is done? What is out of scope? Claude can copy-edit the answers. Claude cannot generate them — because generating the intent is the work. If you cannot answer the five questions, you do not yet have a project.
beats.new_visual_element: Intent layer — five questions / human voice / cannot be delegated / no answers = no project

beats.narration_text: The three files are not overhead. They are the design session made durable. When the same Claude prompt that returned a generic agent returns a specific, constrained, opinionated one — the difference is entirely in what the human wrote first. Maximally informed, minimally autonomous is the Brutalist target: Claude with full context executes your decisions, not its defaults.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — no files: generic output / three files: specific constrained output / human choices visible

beats.narration_text: Write the intent layer before your next creative build. Paste this.

props.command: Before I show you anything I have built, answer these five questions — not generically, in my voice: (1) Who is this for, specifically? (2) What should they feel when they use it? (3) What does this explicitly refuse to do? (4) What does done look like — what would I check? (5) What is out of scope? Do not generate anything else until I answer all five. I will paste my answers, then we build.

beats.narration_text: If this keeps one creative decision traceable to you, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
